summary: Matched a large batch of ftCommon Attack100/adjacent common-fighter routines in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c, replacing many decomp placeholder markers with C implementations and updating ftCo_Attack100.h prototypes from UNK signatures to typed Fighter/Fighter_GObj signatures. The matched area includes item scope start/rapid/fire/end transitions, catch/grab checks, Link/Young Link hookshot catch handling, Samus capture cleanup, catch wait IASA, capture pulled/wait/damage helpers, and callback/motion-state wiring. Review mainly corrected pragma push/pop misuse and discouraged inline assembly in favor of C now that objdiff workflows make whole-file inline asm less necessary.
what_changed: Replaced many `/// #...` placeholders in ftCo_Attack100.c with C functions, with the PR showing 888 added and 85 deleted lines across 30 hunks in the C file.
Added additional dependencies/includes used by the new matches, including ftCommon/types.h, itlinkhookshot.h, it/inlines.h, mp/mplib.h, baselib gobj/jobj/random, and ftLink init headers.

Added local struct overlays for fields needed to match behavior, including `FighterOverlay`, `ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs`, `ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs`, and an inline `ItemScopeVars` cast pattern for scope-related fighter fields.

Implemented item scope flow helpers and callbacks such as `ft_800D76B8`, `ft_800D7770`, `fn_800D7830`, `fn_800D78B0`, `fn_800D7938`, `fn_800D7BDC`, `fn_800D7CEC`, `fn_800D7D70`, `ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_Anim`, `ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Anim`, `fn_800D80F4`, `fn_800D8140`, `fn_800D8268`, `fn_800D82EC`, `fn_800D874C`, `fn_800D87C0`, and `fn_800D8838`.

Implemented catch/grab and tether-related logic including `ftCo_800D8AE0`, `ftCo_800D8B9C`, `fn_800D9228`, `fn_800D949C`, `ftCo_800D94D8`, and `fn_800D9C64`, with explicit Link/Young Link and Samus item cleanup paths.

Implemented capture/capture-damage helpers visible in the diff such as `ftCo_CatchWait_IASA`, `fn_800DAC78`, `fn_800DAEEC`, `ftCo_CapturePulledLw_Phys`, `fn_800DB230`, `ftCo_800DB368`, `ftCo_800DB500`, `fn_800DB790`, `fn_800DBAE4`, `fn_800DBBF8`, `ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys`, `fn_800DBED4`, `ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys`, and `fn_800DC624`.
Changed `fn_800D949C` from a no-argument static declaration to `static void fn_800D949C(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` and updated a call site in `fn_800D8E30` to pass the gobj.

Updated ftCo_Attack100.h prototypes, replacing several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with concrete signatures such as `bool ftCo_800D72A0(Fighter* fp)`, `void fn_800D7938(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`, `void fn_800D80F4(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`, `void fn_800D874C(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`, `void fn_800DB6C8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`, and `void fn_800DBED4(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
smart_moves: Kept unknown data layout localized by using temporary overlay structs and offset-based field names instead of overgeneralizing global Fighter/dat_attrs types from limited evidence.
Matched repeated ground/air item-scope transitions by preserving callback assignments (`accessory4_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`) and motion-state IDs close to the original control flow.
Used explicit character kind checks for Link/Young Link and Samus where catch/tether cleanup differs, rather than forcing a generic catch path.
Updated the header declarations alongside the C implementations, improving type information for future call sites and reducing `UNK_*` usage.
Responded to review by removing empty or imbalanced pragma ranges rather than leaving compiler-control noise around matched functions.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave empty `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` ranges in matched code; reviewers flagged them as confusing and unnecessary.
When using `#pragma dont_inline`, keep push/pop ranges balanced and visually near the functions they affect; stray pushes or pops are easy to miss in large matching PRs.
Inline assembly should be avoided for ordinary game-code matches when a C match is possible; reviewer noted it was common before objdiff but is now generally unnecessary outside SDK code.
Typed header cleanup is part of matching work: once a function body is understood, replace `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` with the observed return type and argument types.

Local overlay structs with padded unknown regions are acceptable evidence-grounded tactics for accessing late Fighter/dat_attr fields during incremental decomp, but field names should remain offset-like until semantics are known.

Model-generated matches may overuse `temp` variable names; the author explicitly noted dislike of excessive `temp` names, so future cleanup should prefer meaningful names where evidence supports them.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as `fn_800D7830`, `fn_800D80F4`, and `ftCo_800D8AE0`.
Known common-fighter motion callbacks follow the module/action suffix style, e.g. `ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_Anim`, `ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Anim`, `ftCo_CatchWait_IASA`, and `ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys`.
Temporary or layout-only structs used offset-style fields such as `x234C`, `x9C`, `xA0`, `xBC`, and padding members like `pad_0`/`pad_A4`.
Character-specific dat_attr overlay names used the `ftCo_<Character>CatchAttrs` pattern for `ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs` and `ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs`.
Header comments preserve address annotations such as `/* 0D7938 */` before prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push` / `#pragma dont_inline on` around some functions to influence matching, but review required removing empty or imbalanced pragma ranges.
Reviewer explicitly rejected adding inline assembly for `ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys`, saying inline asm is no longer necessary outside SDK in the current objdiff workflow.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` and volatile/unused padding in some functions to reproduce stack layout where needed.
Used casts from extern integer symbols to floats, e.g. `*(f32*) &ftCo_804D9020`, to preserve constant/data access patterns seen in the original.
Used local temporaries like `temp_r30`, `temp_r31`, and intermediate `Fighter_GObj*` variables to influence register allocation and match compiler output.
Converted previous inline-wrapper style for some capture damage helpers into direct function bodies where the diff shows inline wrappers being removed or replaced.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Asked why there was an empty pragma range that did nothing.

review_feedback.author: stephenjayakar
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged learning what the pragmas meant and said the empty ranges would be removed.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged a `#pragma pop` that appeared to have no associated push.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged a `#pragma push` with no following `#pragma pop`.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Requested not to use inline assembly, noting it was common pre-objdiff but is generally unnecessary now outside SDK.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename address-based functions in ftCo_Attack100.c after PR #2231.
Search for subsequent cleanup of `ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs` and `ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs` into shared dat_attr headers.
Check whether remaining `/// #` placeholders in ftCo_Attack100.c were matched after this PR.
Look for project-wide review policy discussions about avoiding inline assembly outside SDK code.
Search for later replacements of `temp_r30`/`temp_r31` model-generated variable names with semantic names.
